title: RFE: Support array format for config --cmd
body: `buildah config` does not support the more precise array format for `--cmd` that it does for `entrypoint`, however both [docker](https://docs.docker.com/engine/reference/builder/#cmd) and the OCI [spec](https://github.com/opencontainers/image-spec/blob/master/config.md) support, and even encourage the more explicit array syntax. It would be nice if we could use all the various format combinations for both `--cmd` and for `--entrypoint`.

comments.body: @computator thanks for the issue.  Just to make sure we're on the same page, can you expound a bit with an example or two please?

String format leaves word splitting up to buildah and works with both `--entrypoint` and `--cmd`:
- `--entrypoint /entrypoint.sh`
- `--entrypoint '/entrypoint.sh arg1 "arg two"'`

The more precise array syntax does not do word splitting, instead using the parameters exactly as specified. This format works with both `CMD` and `ENTRYPOINT` in Dockerfiles but buildah only supports it for `--entrypoint`:
- `--entrypoint '[ "/entrypoint.sh" ]'`
- `--entrypoint '[ "/entrypoint.sh", "arg1", "arg two" ]'`

In addition to providing more precision, the array format for `--cmd` potentially has other ramifications. From the Docker `CMD` docs:
> Note: If CMD is used to provide default arguments for the ENTRYPOINT instruction, both the CMD and ENTRYPOINT instructions should be specified with the JSON array format.
>
>Note: The exec form is parsed as a JSON array, which means that you must use double-quotes (“) around words not single-quotes (‘).
>
>Note: Unlike the shell form, the exec form does not invoke a command shell. This means that normal shell processing does not happen. For example, `CMD [ "echo", "$HOME" ]` will not do variable substitution on $HOME. If you want shell processing then either use the shell form or execute a shell directly, for example: `CMD [ "sh", "-c", "echo $HOME" ]`. When using the exec form and executing a shell directly, as in the case for the shell form, it is the shell that is doing the environment variable expansion, not docker.

Entrypoint in particular has extra ramifications based on the format: https://docs.docker.com/engine/reference/builder/#understand-how-cmd-and-entrypoint-interact
@TomSweeneyRedHat Wasn't this just fixed in libpod?  Can we back port the fix from their or better yet share the code?

@computator thanks a bunch for the very thorough follow up.
@rhatdan I don't recall that this was fixed in libpod recently, but so many PR's as of late it could well have been.
I just ran into this again and thought I would check to see if there was any news on the possible backport, or if there was any other news.
@computator I'm afraid no further news, this ones admitedly been suffering some bit rot.
@ashley-cui PTAL, I am not sure if this is fixed or not.

I just hit this tonight while converting a Dockerfile into buildah command script.

Does buildah repo have a 30-day nag bot?
Nope, but we have one for Podman, would be nice to get one here.
